title: Municipality

infobox.value: is usually a single [administrative division](/wiki/Administrative_division "Administrative division") having [corporate](/wiki/Municipal_corporation "Municipal corporation") status and powers of [self-government](/wiki/Self-government "Self-government") or [jurisdiction](/wiki/Jurisdiction "Jurisdiction") as granted by national and regional laws to which it is subordinate.

([France](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France"), French-speaking Belgium and Switzerland, French-speaking Africa, e.g. [Benin](/wiki/Communes_of_Benin "Communes of Benin")), in Italian **[comune](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/comune#Italian "wikt:comune")** ([Italy](/wiki/Comune "Comune"), Italian-speaking Switzerland, e.g. [Ticino](/wiki/Municipalities_of_the_canton_of_Ticino "Municipalities of the canton of Ticino")), in Portuguese **[comuna](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/comuna#Portuguese "wikt:comuna")** ([Angola](/wiki/Communes_of_Angola "Communes of Angola")), in Romanian **[comună](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/comun%C4%83#Romanian "wikt:comună")** ([Romania](/wiki/Communes_of_Romania "Communes of Romania")), and in Spanish **[comuna](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/comuna#Spanish "wikt:comuna")** ([Chile](/wiki/Communes_of_Chile "Communes of Chile"));

([Faroe Islands](/wiki/Municipalities_of_the_Faroe_Islands "Municipalities of the Faroe Islands")), and in Swedish **[kommun](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/kommun#Swedish "wikt:kommun")** ([Sweden](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Sweden "Municipalities of Sweden"), [Finland](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Finland "Municipalities of Finland"));

([Netherlands](/wiki/Municipalities_of_the_Netherlands "Municipalities of the Netherlands"), Dutch-speaking areas of Belgium), Luxembourgish **[Gemeng](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/Gemeng#Luxembourgish "wikt:Gemeng")** ([Luxembourg](/wiki/Communes_of_Luxembourg "Communes of Luxembourg")) and German **[Gemeinde](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/Gemeinde#German "wikt:Gemeinde")** ([Austria](/wiki/Municipality_(Austria) "Municipality (Austria)"), [Germany](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Germany "Municipalities of Germany"));

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Local government area

A **municipality** is usually a single [administrative division](/wiki/Administrative_division "Administrative division") having [corporate](/wiki/Municipal_corporation "Municipal corporation") status and powers of [self-government](/wiki/Self-government "Self-government") or [jurisdiction](/wiki/Jurisdiction "Jurisdiction") as granted by national and regional laws to which it is subordinate.

The term *municipality* may also mean the governing body of a given municipality.[[1]](#cite_note-1) A municipality is a general-purpose administrative subdivision, as opposed to a [special-purpose district](/wiki/Special_district_(United_States) "Special district (United States)").

The [English](/wiki/English_language "English language") word is derived from [French](/wiki/French_language "French language") *municipalité*, which in turn derives from the [Latin](/wiki/Latin_language "Latin language") *municipalis*,[[2]](#cite_note-2) based on the word for [social contract](/wiki/Social_contract "Social contract") (*[municipium](/wiki/Municipium "Municipium")*), referring originally to the Latin communities that supplied Rome with troops in exchange for their own incorporation into the Roman state (granting [Roman citizenship](/wiki/Roman_citizenship "Roman citizenship") to the inhabitants) while permitting the communities to retain their own local governments (a limited autonomy).

A municipality can be any political [jurisdiction](/wiki/Jurisdiction_(area) "Jurisdiction (area)"), from a sovereign state such as the [Principality of Monaco](/wiki/Principality_of_Monaco "Principality of Monaco"), to a small village such as [West Hampton Dunes, New York](/wiki/West_Hampton_Dunes,_New_York "West Hampton Dunes, New York").

The territory over which a municipality has jurisdiction may encompass:

* only one [populated place](/wiki/Populated_place "Populated place") such as a city, [town](/wiki/Town "Town"), or [village](/wiki/Village "Village")
* several such places (e.g., early jurisdictions in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [New Jersey](/wiki/New_Jersey "New Jersey") (1798–1899) as [townships](/wiki/Township_(New_Jersey) "Township (New Jersey)") governing several villages, [municipalities of Mexico](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Mexico "Municipalities of Mexico"), [municipalities of Colombia](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Colombia "Municipalities of Colombia"))
* only parts of such places, sometimes [boroughs](/wiki/Boroughs "Boroughs") of a [city](/wiki/City "City"), such as the 34 municipalities of [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago "Santiago"), Chile.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Political powers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Municipality&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Political powers")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a2/Ponce_City_Hall.JPG/250px-Ponce_City_Hall.JPG)](/wiki/File:Ponce_City_Hall.JPG)

The [Ponce City Hall](/wiki/Ponce_City_Hall "Ponce City Hall"), in the city of [Ponce, Puerto Rico](/wiki/Ponce,_Puerto_Rico "Ponce, Puerto Rico"), is the seat of the government for both the city and the surrounding *[barrios](/wiki/Barrios_of_Puerto_Rico "Barrios of Puerto Rico")* making up the municipality.

Powers of municipalities range from virtual autonomy to complete subordination to the [state](/wiki/State_(polity) "State (polity)"). Municipalities may have the right to tax individuals and corporations with [income tax](/wiki/Income_tax "Income tax"), [property tax](/wiki/Property_tax "Property tax"), and [corporate income tax](/wiki/Corporate_income_tax "Corporate income tax"), but may also receive substantial funding from the state. In some [European](/wiki/Europe "Europe") countries, such as Germany, municipalities have the constitutional right to [supply public services](/wiki/Public_service "Public service") through municipally owned [public utility companies](/wiki/Public_utility "Public utility").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Terms in various countries

[New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City")'s composite five [boroughs](/wiki/Boroughs_of_New_York_City "Boroughs of New York City") are all counties containing historical municipalities.

1. [Manhattan](/wiki/Manhattan "Manhattan")

2. [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn")

3. [Queens](/wiki/Queens "Queens")

4. [The Bronx](/wiki/The_Bronx "The Bronx")

5. [Staten Island](/wiki/Staten_Island "Staten Island")

In 1898, all of the municipalities within the five boroughs were merged into one municipality, and the five counties became boroughs of the new New York City municipality. The five boroughs still have statuses as counties, through.

In [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"), both the first-level administrative divisions and the second-level administrative divisions are municipalities. Norway has 15 first-level municipalities (which are shown in this image). They're called *fylkeskommuner* (county municipalities) and share borders with the country's [counties](/wiki/County "County"). They're further divided into 357 second-level municipalities called *primærkommuner* (primary municipalities) or just *kommuner* (municipalities).

### Municipality

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Municipality&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Municipality")]

Terms cognate with "municipality", mostly referring to territory or political structure,[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] are Spanish **[municipio](/wiki/Municipio "Municipio")** (Spain) and **[municipalidad](/wiki/Municipalidad "Municipalidad")** (Chile), Catalan **[municipi](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/municipi#Catalan "wikt:municipi")**, Portuguese *município*.

* In [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"), a *[município](/wiki/Munic%C3%ADpio "Município")* is the local government, recognized by the Brazilian Federal Constitution and established through state constitutions. It is the smallest territorial division holding executive and legislative powers. Since the [Constitution of 1988](/wiki/Constitution_of_Brazil "Constitution of Brazil"), all *municípios* are members of the federation. Colloquially, the local population uses the terms *municipality* and *city* interchangeably, although the constitution defines "city" as the seat of the municipality.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Commune

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Municipality&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Commune")]

In many countries, terms cognate with "commune" are used, referring to the community living in the area and the common interest. These include terms:

* in [Romance languages](/wiki/Romance_languages "Romance languages"), such as in French **[commune](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/commune#French "wikt:commune")** ([France](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France"), French-speaking Belgium and Switzerland, French-speaking Africa, e.g. [Benin](/wiki/Communes_of_Benin "Communes of Benin")), in Italian **[comune](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/comune#Italian "wikt:comune")** ([Italy](/wiki/Comune "Comune"), Italian-speaking Switzerland, e.g. [Ticino](/wiki/Municipalities_of_the_canton_of_Ticino "Municipalities of the canton of Ticino")), in Portuguese **[comuna](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/comuna#Portuguese "wikt:comuna")** ([Angola](/wiki/Communes_of_Angola "Communes of Angola")), in Romanian **[comună](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/comun%C4%83#Romanian "wikt:comună")** ([Romania](/wiki/Communes_of_Romania "Communes of Romania")), and in Spanish **[comuna](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/comuna#Spanish "wikt:comuna")** ([Chile](/wiki/Communes_of_Chile "Communes of Chile"));
* in [Nordic languages](/wiki/North_Germanic_languages "North Germanic languages") such as in Danish and Norwegian ***[kommune](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/kommune "wikt:kommune")*** ([Denmark](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Denmark "Municipalities of Denmark"), [Norway](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Norway "Municipalities of Norway")), in Faroese **[kommuna](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/kommuna#Faroese "wikt:kommuna")** ([Faroe Islands](/wiki/Municipalities_of_the_Faroe_Islands "Municipalities of the Faroe Islands")), and in Swedish **[kommun](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/kommun#Swedish "wikt:kommun")** ([Sweden](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Sweden "Municipalities of Sweden"), [Finland](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Finland "Municipalities of Finland"));
* in [West Germanic languages](/wiki/West_Germanic_languages "West Germanic languages") such as in Dutch **[gemeente](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/gemeente#Dutch "wikt:gemeente")** ([Netherlands](/wiki/Municipalities_of_the_Netherlands "Municipalities of the Netherlands"), Dutch-speaking areas of Belgium), Luxembourgish **[Gemeng](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/Gemeng#Luxembourgish "wikt:Gemeng")** ([Luxembourg](/wiki/Communes_of_Luxembourg "Communes of Luxembourg")) and German **[Gemeinde](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/Gemeinde#German "wikt:Gemeinde")** ([Austria](/wiki/Municipality_(Austria) "Municipality (Austria)"), [Germany](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Germany "Municipalities of Germany"));
* in [Finnish](/wiki/Finnish_language "Finnish language") **[kunta](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/kunta "wikt:kunta")** ([Finland](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Finland "Municipalities of Finland"));
* in [Ukrainian](/wiki/Ukrainian_language "Ukrainian language") **[hromada](/wiki/Hromada "Hromada")** ([Ukraine](/wiki/List_of_hromadas_of_Ukraine "List of hromadas of Ukraine")); and
* in [Polish](/wiki/Polish_language "Polish language") **[gmina](/wiki/Gmina "Gmina")** ([Poland](/wiki/Gmina "Gmina")).

The same terms "Gemeente" (Dutch) or "Gemeinde" (German) may be used for church congregations or parishes, for example, in the German and Dutch Protestant churches.

### Other terms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Municipality&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Other terms")]

In Greece, the word Δήμος (*demos*) is used, also meaning 'community'; the word is known in English from the compound *democracy* (rule of the people).

In some countries, the Spanish term **[ayuntamiento](/wiki/Ayuntamiento "Ayuntamiento")**, referring to a municipality's administration building, is extended via [synecdoche](/wiki/Synecdoche "Synecdoche") to denote the municipality itself.[[6]](#cite_note-6) In [Moldova](/wiki/Communes_of_Moldova "Communes of Moldova") and [Romania](/wiki/Communes_of_Romania "Communes of Romania"), both *municipalities* (*[municipiu](/wiki/Municipiu "Municipiu")*; urban administrative units) and *communes* (*[comună](/wiki/Comun%C4%83 "Comună")*; rural units) exist, and a commune may be part of a municipality.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In many countries, comparable entities may exist with various names.

### English-speaking

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Municipality&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: English-speaking")]

* In [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"), the term [local government area](/wiki/Local_government_area "Local government area") (LGA) is used in place of the generic municipality. Here, the "LGA Structure covers only incorporated areas of Australia. Incorporated areas are legally designated parts of states and territories over which incorporated local governing bodies have responsibility."[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* In [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), municipalities are local governments established through provincial and territorial legislation, usually within general municipal statutes.[[8]](#cite_note-CDNEncyclo-8)[[9]](#cite_note-StatCan-9) Types of [municipalities within Canada](/wiki/Lists_of_municipalities_in_Canada "Lists of municipalities in Canada") include [cities](/wiki/City "City"), [district municipalities](/wiki/District_municipality "District municipality"), [municipal districts](/wiki/Municipal_district "Municipal district"), municipalities, [parishes](/wiki/Parish_(administrative_division) "Parish (administrative division)"), [rural municipalities](/wiki/Rural_municipality "Rural municipality"), [towns](/wiki/Town "Town"), [townships](/wiki/Township "Township"), [villages](/wiki/Village "Village"), and [villes](/wiki/Ville "Ville") among others.[[9]](#cite_note-StatCan-9) The province of [Ontario](/wiki/Ontario "Ontario") has different tiers of municipalities, including lower, upper, and single tiers.[[10]](#cite_note-OntarioMAH-10) Types of upper tier municipalities in Ontario include counties and regional municipalities.[[10]](#cite_note-OntarioMAH-10) [Nova Scotia](/wiki/Nova_Scotia "Nova Scotia") also has regional municipalities, which include cities, counties, districts, or towns as municipal units.[[11]](#cite_note-NSMGA-11)
* In [India](/wiki/India "India"), a municipality (also known as *[municipal council](/wiki/Municipal_council_(India) "Municipal council (India)")*) is an urban local body that administers a city of population 100,000 or more (the criteria varies from state to state). However, there are exceptions to that, as previously municipalities were constituted in urban centers with population over 20,000, so all the urban bodies which were previously classified as municipality were reclassified as municipality even if their population was under 100,000. it interacts directly with the state government, though it is administratively part of the [district](/wiki/District#India "District") it is located in. Generally, smaller district cities and bigger towns have a municipality. Municipalities are also a form of local self-government entrusted with some duties and responsibilities, as enshrined in the [Constitutional (74th Amendment) Act,1992](/wiki/Seventy-fourth_Amendment_of_the_Constitution_of_India "Seventy-fourth Amendment of the Constitution of India").
* In the [United Kingdom](/wiki/Local_government_in_the_United_Kingdom "Local government in the United Kingdom"), the term was used until the [Local Government Act 1972](/wiki/1972_Local_Government_Act "1972 Local Government Act") came into effect in 1974 in [England](/wiki/England "England") and [Wales](/wiki/Wales "Wales"), and until 1975 in [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland") and 1976 in [Northern Ireland](/wiki/Northern_Ireland "Northern Ireland"), "both for a city or town which is organized for self-government under a municipal corporation, and also for the governing body itself. Such a corporation in Great Britain consists of a head as a mayor or provost, and of superior members, as aldermen and councillors".[[12]](#cite_note-12) Since local government reorganisation, the unit in England, Northern Ireland and Wales is known as a *[district](/wiki/District "District")*, and in Scotland as a *[council area](/wiki/Council_area "Council area")*. A *district* may be awarded *[borough](/wiki/Borough "Borough")* or *city* status, or can retain its *district* title.
* In [Jersey](/wiki/Jersey "Jersey"), a municipality refers to the honorary officials elected to run each of the 12 [parishes](/wiki/Parishes_of_Jersey "Parishes of Jersey") into which it is subdivided. This is the highest level of regional government in this jurisdiction.
* In [Trinidad and Tobago](/wiki/Trinidad_and_Tobago "Trinidad and Tobago"), "municipality" is usually understood as a city, town, or other local government unit, formed by municipal charter from the state as a municipal corporation. A town may be awarded borough status and, later on, may be upgraded to city status. [Chaguanas](/wiki/Chaguanas "Chaguanas"), [San Fernando](/wiki/San_Fernando,_Trinidad_and_Tobago "San Fernando, Trinidad and Tobago"), [Port of Spain](/wiki/Port_of_Spain "Port of Spain"), [Arima](/wiki/Arima "Arima") and [Point Fortin](/wiki/Point_Fortin "Point Fortin") are the 5 current municipalities in Trinidad and Tobago.
* In the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), "municipality" is usually understood as a city, town, village, or other local government unit, formed by municipal charter from the state as a municipal corporation.[[13]](#cite_note-13) In a state law context, some U.S. state codes define "municipality" more widely, from the state itself to any political subdivisions given jurisdiction over an area that may include multiple populated places and unpopulated places[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) (see also: [Local government in the United States#Municipal governments](/wiki/Local_government_in_the_United_States#Municipal_governments "Local government in the United States")).

### Chinese-speaking

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Municipality&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Chinese-speaking")]

* In the [People's Republic of China](/wiki/China "China"), a [direct-administered municipality](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_China "Administrative divisions of China") (直辖市 in [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): zhíxiáshì) is a municipality with equal status to a [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_China "Provinces of China"): [Beijing Municipality](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing"), [Chongqing Municipality](/wiki/Chongqing "Chongqing"), [Shanghai Municipality](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai"), and [Tianjin Municipality](/wiki/Tianjin "Tianjin") (see also: [Direct-administered municipalities of China](/wiki/Direct-administered_municipalities_of_China "Direct-administered municipalities of China")).
* In the [Republic of China (Taiwan)](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan"), a [special municipality](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_Taiwan "Administrative divisions of Taiwan") (直轄市 in [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): zhíxiáshì) is a municipality with equal status to a province: [Kaohsiung](/wiki/Kaohsiung "Kaohsiung"), [New Taipei](/wiki/New_Taipei_City "New Taipei City"), [Taichung](/wiki/Taichung "Taichung"), [Tainan](/wiki/Tainan "Tainan"), [Taipei](/wiki/Taipei "Taipei"), and [Taoyuan](/wiki/Taoyuan,_Taiwan "Taoyuan, Taiwan") (see also: [Special municipality (Taiwan)](/wiki/Special_municipality_(Taiwan) "Special municipality (Taiwan)")).

* In Portuguese language usage, there are two words to distinguish the territory and the administrative organ. When referring to the territory, the word ***[concelho](/wiki/Concelho "Concelho")*** is used, when referring to the [organ of State](/wiki/Organs_of_state "Organs of state"), the word ***município*** is used. This differentiation is in use in Portugal and some of its former overseas provinces, but it is no longer in use in Brazil, where ***município*** refers to the territorial boundaries and ***prefeitura*** is its administrative organ.
